document_profile.domain: Government Financial Services, Treasury Operations, Check Processing
document_profile.workflow: Check Capture, Deposit Processing, Card Processing, Reporting, User Role Management
document_profile.purpose: To define system elements, process flows, and user roles for agencies and financial institutions interacting with the US Treasury.
document_profile.audience: Agency Users, Financial Institution (FI) Staff, Federal Reserve Bank (FRB) Staff, System Administrators
document_profile.content_structure: Hierarchical sections covering Introduction, Process Flows (Figures), User Roles (Tables), and System Tasks.

domain_context: The document describes 'OTCnet', a system for collecting US and foreign funds (checks, cash, cards) for the Treasury.
Key workflows involve converting paper checks to electronic debits (Check 21), processing card payments via kiosks, and reporting deposits.
The ecosystem involves three main participant types: Agencies (collectors), Financial Institutions (FI) (where checks are drawn), and Federal Reserve Banks (FRB) (settlement/clearing).
Data flows from OTCnet to the Collections Information Repository (CIR), then to the Treasury Cash Management System (TCMS), and finally to the Central Accounting Reporting System (CARS).
style_guidance: Maintain strict distinction between 'Agency', 'FI' (Financial Institution), and 'FRB' (Federal Reserve Bank) roles.
Preserve the distinction between 'Check Capture' (imaging) and 'Check Processing' (transaction execution).
Ensure 'PLSA' (Primary) and 'LSA' (Local) are clearly differentiated as they denote hierarchy.
Acronyms like CIR (Repository) and CIRA (Archive) refer to different systems and must not be conflated.
Tables define 'Combinable' roles; ensure the concept of role combination is clear in translation.
